title: elymus repens subsp. arenosus
intro: Elymus repens subsp. arenosus, also known as crested dog's tail, is a subspecies of the Elymus repens plant, a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Elymus repens subsp. arenosus include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdered them for use in capsules or topical creams.
